title: Advancing GLOF hazard assessment in Bhutan
summary: New NHESS study finds over 11,000 people exposed to glacial lake outburst flood risk in Bhutan through hydrodynamic flood mapping and exposure analysis. Simon Allen co-authored.

title: GLOF risk modelling advances in Kyrgyz Republic
summary: New research published in NHESS compares lake lowering, deflection dams, and retention basins for managing glacial lake outburst flood risk in the Ala-Archa valley.

title: New study: Mitigating future glacial lake outburst floods in the Himalaya
summary: Published in Science Bulletin, this study evaluates China's Glacial Lake Management System and finds that GLMS engineering could reduce flood intensity by 24%. Simon Allen contributed as co-author.

title: TopoPyScale v0.3.3 released with critical bug fix
summary: TopoPyScale v0.3.3 fixes a critical bug where clustered pixels were not remapped to their correct locations, causing incorrect spatial output when using the clustering option.

title: TopoPyScale v0.3.1 released
summary: Latest release of our open-source Python library for hillslope-scale climate downscaling, now used by research groups worldwide for snow, permafrost, and hydrological modelling.

title: WMO Global Cryosphere Watch: Regional Snow Assessments 2025
summary: Joel Fiddes contributed to the GCW 2025 regional snow assessments, providing operational snow analysis for Central Asia as part of the WMO expert network.

title: CROMO-ADAPT enters final phase
summary: The CHF 2.96M SDC-funded project on cryospheric observation and modelling in Central Asia enters its final implementation phase, with operational tools now serving national hydrometeorological agencies.

title: SnowMapper completes second operational season
summary: SnowMapper CA completed its second full winter season covering 400,000+ km² across five Central Asian countries. The 2024–25 assessment documented anomalously low snow in the Syr Darya basin.
